title: helix-cli: Staging Merge Conflict Resolution (BLD-448)
description: This tour walks through the conflict resolution run for ticket BLD-448 (Library Comments and Iteration) in the helix-cli repository. A staging refresh introduced a reported merge conflict in src/tickets/index.ts between the ticket branch (library CLI integration, 4 commits) and the staging branch (concurrent ticket command updates, 1 commit). The conflict was auto-resolved cleanly with no markers remaining. This run verified the resolution, confirmed all 10 subcommand dispatchers are intact, and validated the TypeScript build passes with zero errors. No source code changes were required.

steps.description: The diagnosis statement explains the trigger: a staging refresh flagged src/tickets/index.ts as conflicted between 4 ticket commits (library CLI implementation and prior ticket lookup changes) and 1 staging commit (concurrent ticket command updates). The root cause was concurrent modifications to the ticket command dispatcher. Crucially, the diagnosis immediately established that the conflict was PRE-RESOLVED — zero markers remained — so the entire run's purpose shifted from 'fix conflicts' to 'verify the auto-resolution is correct and the build passes'.
steps.title: Overview: Why This Run Exists

steps.description: The APL (Artifact-Producing Loop) for diagnosis answered three questions. Q1: Grep for '<<<<<<<', '=======', '>>>>>>>' in src/tickets/index.ts returned zero matches. Q2: A broad grep across all src/ .ts files also returned zero source-level hits — the only 16 hits were in .helix/ artifact JSON files from previous runs. Q3: The 150-line resolved file was inspected and confirmed to contain all expected subcommand imports (list, latest, get, create, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, update-description) and a complete switch statement. This evidence chain justified concluding the merge was clean.
steps.title: Diagnosis: Verifying Zero Conflict Markers

steps.description: The implementation plan codified the 'no code changes needed' decision into four governing principles: (1) No code changes — conflicts already resolved. (2) Verify, don't re-implement — confirm via build. (3) Minimal touch — only the 1 file in merge-conflicts.json is in scope. (4) Build is the gate — TypeScript compilation is the primary correctness signal. The three implementation steps were: grep verification for 0 markers, npm install for dependencies, and npm run build (tsc) for the TypeScript gate. This plan correctly deferred build verification to the implementation step since it cannot be done at planning time.
steps.title: Implementation Plan: Verify-Only Strategy

steps.description: The implementation plan APL explicitly flagged Q3 (will the TypeScript build pass?) as 'Expected yes based on file integrity inspection, but build has not yet been executed — deferred to implementation/verification step.' This honest deferral is important: it prevents the planning phase from making untested claims about build success, and ensures the implementation step actually runs the compiler rather than assuming correctness from static inspection alone.
steps.title: Implementation Plan APL: Build Gate Acknowledged

steps.description: The implementation APL confirmed the final outcome: merge-conflicts.json listed src/tickets/index.ts, but the file contained no conflict markers when inspected. Grep for conflict markers in src/tickets/index.ts returned no matches, and a broad grep across all *.ts files also returned no matches. No code changes were made. The statement records: 'Conflict resolution run for helix-cli completed. No actual conflict markers were present in the listed file or anywhere else in the repository.'
steps.title: Implementation: Conflict Resolution Confirmed Complete

steps.description: The code review independently verified three things without relying on the implementation agent's assertions: (1) src/tickets/index.ts — 150 lines, no conflict markers, all 10 subcommand imports at lines 4-13, complete switch statement with all 10 cases, resolveTicket properly used for ref-based commands. (2) All src/ files — grep for conflict markers returned zero hits. (3) Library comment feature files — all 6 files (library/comments-list.ts, library/comments-post.ts, library/comments.ts, library/index.ts, library/show.ts, src/index.ts) present with library comment references. Verdict: No code changes needed, merge integrity confirmed.
steps.title: Code Review: Independent Verification of Merge Integrity

steps.description: The code review APL provides the final summary statement for the entire run: 'The helix-cli merge from staging was auto-resolved cleanly. No conflict markers remain, all 10 subcommands are preserved in tickets/index.ts including the ticket-side library commands, the TypeScript build passes with tsc --noEmit exit code 0, and no code changes were needed from code review.' The three gates — (1) merge integrity via grep, (2) functional completeness via subcommand inspection, and (3) type correctness via tsc — all passed. The codebase's major code paths through src/tickets/index.ts and src/library/ remain clean and fully operational.
steps.title: Summary: All Three Verification Gates Passed
